title: Eagles secure Currie Cup semi-finals spot
description: GEORGE NEWS - The Phangela SWD Eagles are...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The Phangela SWD Eagles are through to the Carling Currie Cup First Division semi-finals after beating the Leopards 33-45 on their home turf at Olen Park in Potchefstroom. At half-time the score was even at 19-19 and a total of 12 tries were scored during the match.

The Eagles narrowly sneaked into fourth position on the log past the Leopards thanks to their points difference.


> Both the Eagles and Leopards have two wins and three losses with 12 points on the log, but the Eagles' -21 points difference places them above the Leopards who has -79.


The semi-final will take place on Saturday 20 July when the Eagles take on log leaders Boland Kavaliers in Wellington. It will be a tough game for the Eagles who will have to improve on their previous performance against the Kavaliers which they lost 43-7.

Scorers

SWD

Tries (7): Damon Royle; Sachin Toring (2), Thoubaan Gabriels, Clive Kruger (2), JP Duvenage.

Conversions: Sachin Toring, Marlo Weich (4).

Leopards

Tries (5): Kowie Roos, Edward White, Khwezi Junior Mafu, Siniothando Tshontsyi, Brandon Keevy.

Conversions: Charles Williams (4)


> Individual performances of SWD Eagles in the Currie Cup: Rosco Syster has the 6th most tries (4). Sachin Toring has the 6th most conversions (7).


‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
